steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content:
Each term has a factor of $x^4$. This is the greatest common power of $x$ shared by all three terms:
$$8x^6-3x^5+5x^4$$

steps.title: Factor out the common power
steps.content:
Divide each term by $x^4$ to get the expression inside the parentheses, keeping the original signs and coefficients:
$$8x^6-3x^5+5x^4=x^4(8x^2-3x+5)$$

steps.title: Match the factored form
steps.content: The equivalent expression is $x^4(8x^2-3x+5)$, which expands back to all three original terms. The answer is B.
